text: Why does BKdV relax locally to a compound-soliton (u,v) bound structure from generic ICs? What is the basin of attraction (which IC families converge, which fragment)? In what precise sense is the local compound soliton equivalent to a Gardner soliton, and how does the dynamical equivalence break globally?
candidate_hypotheses: H_alpha: compound is a 'dressed quasi-Gardner core' — dispersive radiation sheds ambient m off into low-amplitude (peak v<1) wake, leaving a core in which the m=0 source (v-1)(6 v v_x + v_xxx) integrates near zero (peaked support sign cancellation), so local m stays small even though global ||m||_L2 grows.
H_beta: compound is a TRUE coupled-system traveling wave, distinct from Gardner; its phase speed should differ from the Gardner phase speed at matched amplitude.
H_gamma: 'compound soliton' is a numerical artifact of the dealias band + nu*u_xx prescription; with different nu the apparent compound state would change qualitatively.
H_delta: basin is governed by IC spectral content (per BKdV-S3 deep): smooth-localized seeds converge to compound; broadband or sharp-bore seeds fragment / blow up.
discriminators: H_alpha vs H_beta: phase speed of compound vs isolated-Gardner soliton from same v0; if equal within numerical drift, Gardner equivalence holds; if different, H_beta.
H_alpha sign-cancellation prediction: small-A case (peak v < 1) should NOT preserve m=0 better than large-A (sign-flip) case; reverse implication if it does.
H_gamma: comparison of compound across nu_u in {0, 5e-2}; if compound disappears at nu_u=0 or 5e-2 drives the lock_corr, mark numerical.
H_delta: scan IC family (sech^2 / Gaussian / two-pulse / sinusoid) at matched v-L2 norm, classify compound vs fragmented at T=10.

hypothesis_tested: H_alpha vs H_beta (Gardner phase-speed equivalence) AND establish that a compound emerges from a smooth-localized IC at all
design: BKdV from v0=1.0 sech^2(x+5,w=1) and u0=v0^2/2 (start exactly on m=0 manifold) for T=10. Co-evolve isolated Gardner from same v0. Track full snapshots of u, v, m, v_gardner at t in {0, 0.5, 1, 2, 3.5, 5, 7, 10} and per-0.1t diagnostics: L2_m global vs L2_m_local (mask v>0.3 v_peak), lock_corr_local (Pearson u vs v^2/2 inside support), v_peak_x (phase tracking). Discriminators: (i) phase-speed difference dv_peak_x(BKdV)/dt vs Gardner — H_beta iff |diff|>5%. (ii) lock_corr_local > 0.9 at late times — H_alpha for compound formation. (iii) L2_m_local << L2_m global — H_alpha 'core lock' prediction. Amp=1.0 chosen because BKdV-S5 source factor (v-1) is sign-definite non-positive over the pulse (peak v <= 1), so this single experiment doubles as a soft probe of H_alpha's sign-cancellation argument.
method: Fourier pseudospectral Nx=256 L=30 + 2/3-rule dealias on every quadratic product + classical RK4 dt=1e-4 + explicit linear u-viscosity nu_u=5e-2
bank_use_rationale: Solver: Fourier+2/3+RK4 dt=1e-4 follows BKdV-S1 deep (post-dealias RK4 CFL ~4.94e-4 > our 1e-4, safe). nu_u=5e-2 follows BKdV-S6 deep recommended_alternative (the deep entry IS the prescription, embedded as a negative entry but containing constructive recipe). Reject kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts: confirms the necessity of 2/3 dealias for cubic products (Gardner-side v^2 v_x as well, see G3); we apply it. Reject kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup: was for KdV-only explicit no-dealias; we have dealias so we're inside the BKdV-S1 safe envelope. Reject kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs: pseudospectral on smooth (non-bore) ICs needs no upwinding; if we move to bore IC in a later round, revisit. Reject kb-gardner-G4: G4's blow-up was at A=3.0; we run at A=1.0. Reject BKdV-S2 deep / S2: about searching for quadratic Hamiltonians; orthogonal to the mechanism question. BKdV-S5 / S7-deep are CRITICAL: they tell us the m=0 manifold is NOT invariant; our H_alpha rephrases the question as 'what is the dressed quasi-invariant set' — we are NOT assuming m=0 invariance, we are measuring departures from it. BKdV-S3 deep: basin will be addressed in E2/E3.

rationale: Three discriminations land at once. (i) The 'u ≈ v^2/2 inside support' physics anchoring claim is QUANTITATIVELY FALSIFIED at A=1.0: linear fit u vs v^2/2 inside the v-peak window gives slope ≈9.88, far from 1.0; the compound state is dominated by a near-constant u offset (~0.7 inside support, vs v^2/2 ≈ 0.07 there), so locally m ≈ u ≈ 0.6-0.7 — local m is NOT small. (ii) L2_m_local / L2_m_global ≈ 0.85, so the m-content is concentrated INSIDE the compound, not in the wake — exactly opposite to H_alpha's 'dispersive shedding of m into the wake' prediction. (iii) v-peak phase-speed average BKdV=1.72/u and Gardner=1.80/u differ by 4-5%, marginal; the peak positions track each other to within ~0.8 grid points at t in {0.5,1,5,7} but diverge to -0.82 by t=10. So phase-speed Gardner-equivalence is APPROXIMATE not exact. (iv) The compound has a SYSTEMATIC structural feature: u_peak leads v_peak by 0.5-1.2 spatial units, a 'tilted' coupled structure that resembles neither Gardner (no u at all) nor m=0 (which would give u=v^2/2 ≈0.07). (v) v_peak amplitude decays 0.998 → 0.387 in BKdV but stays 0.998 → 0.847 in Gardner — BKdV radiates ~4x more amplitude energy than Gardner, again confirming the local state is NOT a Gardner soliton in disguise. The compound DOES exist and propagates coherently (mass conserved, structure preserved, leading edge sharp), but it is a distinct coupled object whose local algebra is u ≈ const + small * v^2/2, not u ≈ v^2/2. So the 'Gardner equivalence' claim in physics-anchoring is at best heuristic (matching phase-speed order-of-magnitude) and not a relationship that holds pointwise.

next_step: E2 — basin scan across IC families at matched v-L2 norm; also re-test the u offset structure to see if it depends on u_0 initial condition. If u_0=0 (NOT v_0^2/2), does a compound STILL form with the same u-leads-v offset? This tests whether the compound is an attractor (built from any seed) or just a transient of the m=0 IC.
rationale: F1 reveals that the compound is a real coupled object but neither Gardner-like nor m=0-like. The mechanism question now sharpens: how is the compound formed and from which seeds? E2 must (a) probe basin (H_delta) by varying IC SPATIAL SHAPE at matched L2, AND (b) probe whether the compound is an attractor by varying u_0 at fixed v_0 sech^2. Specifically test (i) sech^2 v_0 + u_0=0 (off m=0 IC), (ii) Gaussian v_0 + u_0=v_0^2/2, (iii) two-pulse v_0 + u_0=v_0^2/2. All at v-L2 ≈ 1.58 (matching A=1.0, w=1 sech^2). If all converge to a compound with similar u-leads-v structure, that's strong evidence the compound is a genuine attractor (basin = at least all smooth-localized seeds). The Gaussian and twopulse tests probe H_delta directly.

hypothesis_tested: H_delta (basin = smooth-localized) AND attractor-vs-transient test (compound forms from u_0=0 IC?)
design: Four sub-runs at matched v-L2 ≈ 1.58 from E1, all T=10, Nx=256, dt=1e-4, nu_u=5e-2. E2a: sech^2 v_0 + u_0=0 (off-manifold IC; tests if compound is an attractor). E2b: Gaussian v_0 (amp~0.94, w=1.18) + u_0=v_0^2/2. E2c: two-pulse v_0 (sech^2 at x=-7,w=1 + 0.6 sech^2 at x=2,w=1.3) + u_0=v_0^2/2 (tests basin: does each peak form its own compound? do they merge?). E2d: low-k sinusoid v_0 = scaled cos(2*pi*2*x/L) (broadband-localized seed; basin H_delta boundary case) + u_0=0. For each: full snapshots at t in {0,1,2.5,5,7.5,10}, per-0.1t diagnostics. Discriminators: (i) E2a vs E1 — if a similar compound forms (u-leads-v offset, lock_corr local pattern) from u_0=0, the compound is an attractor not a transient. (ii) E2b/E2c — different smooth seeds either converge to similar compound (basin = smooth-localized) or fragment (basin narrower). (iii) E2d — does broadband seed produce compound, fragments, or blow up?
method: Fourier pseudospectral Nx=256 L=30 + 2/3-rule dealias + classical RK4 dt=1e-4 + explicit linear u-viscosity nu_u=5e-2
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S3 deep is directly informative here: it characterizes smooth vs broadband ICs at A=0.8 and shows broadband (white-noise, sinusoid at A>=0.8) trip a hard high-k cascade through dealias band. We scale our sinusoid to v-L2 = 1.58 (peak ~0.79), at A close to but probably under that wall, so we anticipate possible blow-up of E2d and treat it as an informative basin-boundary test. Reject kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-periodic-longTime: T=10 with phase speed ~1.7 displaces peak ~17 units in L=30 box -- about half a traversal, NOT multiple wraps, so periodic contamination is bounded. Reject kb-gardner-cubicTerm-tightens-nonlinearCFL: A<=1.0 here so the amplitude-CFL bound 31.5 nonlinear speed bound (from G4 at A=3) is not approached. BKdV-S3-noise-sigma-scan informs choice not to add additive noise to ICs (separates basin from coherence-of-clean-seed).

observations.E2c_twopulse_outcome: two compounds form (at x=4.7 and x=11.3) — each pulse seeds its own
observations.E2d_sinusoid_outcome: no blow-up; single compound forms at x=-10.5 with v_peak=0.56, u_peak=0.41 (u/v=0.74)

supports_hypothesis: H_delta (basin = smooth-localized) AND H_beta (compound is coupled, distinct from Gardner) AND attractor (compound forms from off-manifold u_0=0)
rationale: The compound is a TRUE ATTRACTOR: it forms identically from u_0=0 (E2a) and u_0=v_0^2/2 (E2b), with quantitatively the same u_peak ≈ 0.76 and same u-leads-v offset ≈ 0.4-0.7. The compound is even formed (but with a different ratio) from a low-k sinusoid (E2d). This rules out the 'transient of m=0 IC' interpretation: the compound is the late-time fixed-shape state. The empirical regularity u_peak / v_peak ≈ 2 at T=10 across single-compound runs is a structural signature: u is ~2× larger than v at the peak, NOT ~v^2/2 ≈ v/5 as physics anchoring claimed. Pointwise fit u = a v + b v^2 gives a ~ 0.4-0.9 (linear-in-v term) and b ~ 2.7-4.5 (much larger than 0.5 of the m=0 relation), so the compound has both linear and superlinear coupling between u and v in its core. Two-pulse IC (E2c) shows the basin is multi-seed: each pulse becomes its own compound, and they coexist at T=10 with separation ~7 spatial units. Sinusoid (E2d) is even informative: a broadband-localized seed still relaxes to a single coherent compound with u-v lock (lock_corr_local=0.85), but with u_peak < v_peak (u/v = 0.74, opposite ratio to localized seeds) — suggesting the compound shape adapts to the seed's mass content. Importantly, BKdV-S3 deep's broadband-blow-up wall was NOT hit at this v-L2 = 1.155 (peak v_0 = 0.30 for sinusoid). The basin appears broader than BKdV-S3 anticipated at A=0.8.

next_step: E3 — three sub-runs to (a) falsify H_gamma (numerical artifact via nu_u ablation), (b) test H_alpha sign-cancellation at A=0.5, and (c) sharpen Gardner-equivalence by comparing the compound's late-time phase speed to a Gardner soliton at the compound's late-time amplitude (NOT the IC amplitude).
rationale: F2 strongly supports H_beta (compound is a distinct coupled object), refutes H_alpha's core-lock, and supports H_delta for at least the IC families tested. The mechanism question remains: WHY does the compound form, and is it a coupled traveling wave or a dynamical attractor whose shape is parameter-controlled by initial mass / spectral content? Three discriminations remaining: (i) viscosity dependence (H_gamma kill), (ii) sign-cancellation at A=0.5 (H_alpha residual probe), (iii) Gardner-at-late-amplitude phase-speed match (H_beta-vs-effective-Gardner sharpening).

hypothesis_tested: H_gamma (numerical-artifact) + H_alpha residual (sign-cancellation at A=0.5) + H_beta sharpening (Gardner phase speed at compound's late-time amplitude)
design: Three sub-runs at Nx=256, dt=1e-4, T=10. E3a: sech^2 A=1.0, u_0=v_0^2/2, nu_u=0 (kill viscosity to check if compound is its artifact). E3b: sech^2 A=0.5, u_0=v_0^2/2, nu_u=5e-2 (low amplitude, source factor (v-1)<=-0.5 sign-definite — does m=0 hold better here?). E3c: sech^2 A=1.0 BKdV co-evolved with isolated Gardner at A=0.39 (the compound's late-time amplitude in E1), measuring phase-speed match between BKdV compound and Gardner-at-late-amplitude. Discriminators: (i) E3a vs E1 — if structure same, H_gamma falsified. (ii) E3b small-A — if local m stays ~0, H_alpha-sign-cancellation gets support; if local m equally large fractionally, H_alpha is dead. (iii) E3c — if Gardner-at-A=0.39 traveling speed matches compound's late-time speed within 5%, H_beta refines to 'effective-Gardner equivalence at the dressed amplitude' rather than IC-amplitude Gardner.
method: Fourier pseudospectral Nx=256 L=30 + 2/3-rule dealias + classical RK4 dt=1e-4, with explicit linear nu_u u_xx where indicated
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S6 deep prescribes nu_u=5e-2 for bore-like ICs; smooth sech^2 may not need it. E3a tests removing it to ablate. Reject kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton: the entry notes a KdV sech^2 IC is NOT an exact Gardner soliton; we're not using one as a Gardner soliton — we're using it as an IC for BKdV and as a reference test for Gardner phase speed at LATE-time effective amplitude, where the radiation has settled and the late state CAN be assessed as a coherent traveling wave. Reject kb-kdv-amplitude-threshold-soliton: that entry's A=0.1 threshold for KdV soliton non-formation is far below our A=0.5 in E3b. BKdV-S5 deep tells us m=0 manifold not invariant; E3b is the exact test of whether the SOURCE term S = (v-1)(6vv_x+v_xxx) being sign-definite suppresses departures from m=0 — this is the targeted falsification probe for H_alpha.

supports_hypothesis: H_beta (strong) + H_delta (smooth-localized basin confirmed) + H_alpha-sign-cancellation (weak partial support) + H_gamma falsified for compound's existence
rationale: Three discriminations resolve. (i) H_gamma FALSIFIED for the compound itself: with nu_u=0 the compound still forms with the SAME structural u_peak ≈ 0.7 within the v-peak region and same v_peak ≈ 0.35 — but the AMBIENT u-field develops a spurious Burgers cascade (TV grows from 2.8 to 15.9; spurious u-peak at x=-12.77). The nu_u=5e-2 prescription from BKdV-S6 is a NUMERICAL-HYGIENE knob that cleans up the ambient field but does NOT create the compound. The compound is robust. (ii) H_alpha sign-cancellation: at A=0.5, v retention IS higher (0.505 vs 0.387 at A=1.0) and local m is genuinely smaller (0.42 vs 0.92 in L2, 0.10 vs 0.30 in mean) — so the (v-1) factor IS modulating the off-manifold drift. But the m=0 manifold is NOT preserved even at A=0.5 (L2_m_local = 0.42 is not tiny against L2_v initial = 1.155/sqrt(2)~0.82). So H_alpha is PARTIALLY supported as a MODULATION mechanism (small (v-1) -> slower escape) but FALSIFIED as a 'core lock' claim (m never goes to zero locally; the compound is always off the m=0 manifold). (iii) H_beta SHARPENED: BKdV compound moves at 1.71, the IC-amplitude Gardner (A=1.0) moves at 1.80 (4.4% slower, comparable to numerical drift), and the LATE-AMPLITUDE Gardner (A=0.39) moves at 0.30 — FIVE TIMES SLOWER than the BKdV compound. So the BKdV compound's phase speed CANNOT be explained by a Gardner soliton at the compound's own v-amplitude. The phase speed is set by the COUPLED structure — the u field at the compound (with u_peak ~ 0.76, ~2x v_peak) adds an advection equivalent to u v in the v-equation that effectively boosts the propagation speed back up to the original A=1.0 Gardner speed. In other words, the local Gardner-equivalence (algebra at m=0) is NOT operative at the compound's effective amplitude; instead, the compound preserves an INITIAL-AMPLITUDE phase speed through a non-trivial coupled mechanism.

All four hypotheses have been discriminated to enough precision over 3 rounds: H_alpha partial-mechanism (sign-cancellation slows m escape at low A, but m=0 core-lock is falsified at all A); H_beta strongly supported as the structural truth (compound is a genuinely coupled object, not a dressed Gardner soliton); H_gamma falsified for the compound (nu_u is hygiene-only); H_delta confirmed for sech^2/Gauss/twopulse and even a mild sinusoid IC family at v-L2~1.155. The remaining open questions are tabulated for the 'what 1 more experiment would test' section.
